title: author-agent-spec in-body writer.kind=='human' check is redundant with FGAP-134/138 auth-gate at pi-dispatch layer
description: author-agent-spec at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/author-agent-spec-tool.ts performs an in-body `if writer.kind !== 'human' throw` check. Per FGAP-134 (closed 2026-05-29) the canonical per-tool user-auth gate is at the pi-dispatch layer (auth-gate.ts intercepting 14+ Bucket-2 tools); per FGAP-138 (closed 2026-05-29) the auth-gate mutates event.input.writer to the verified terminal-operator identity on confirm. The in-body check is now structural redundancy — the auth-gate already enforces human-authorized invocation and verifies operator identity. The redundant check predates FGAP-134/138 and lingers with docstring framing that still describes it as the primary enforcement.

evidence.reference: Discovered-gap (secondary) #4: 'author-agent-spec writer.kind enforcement: Per FGAP-134 (closed 2026-05-29), author-agent-spec performs an in-body if writer.kind !== human throw check that is now redundant with the canonical auth-gate at pi-dispatch layer. The gate is moved but the redundant in-body check remains, and its docstring still describes it as the primary enforcement (misleading per FGAP-136 closure). Cleanup: remove in-body check or clarify it as defense-in-depth only.'
author-agent-spec-tool source contains the in-body writer.kind enforcement; FGAP-134/138 closure moved the canonical enforcement to packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/auth-gate.ts

impact: Structural redundancy and misleading docstrings. Two enforcement layers exist for the same human-authorization concern; the canonical one (auth-gate) is what actually fires the user-confirm prompt. The in-body check is now a no-op for any invocation that already passed the gate, and is misleading reading for agents debugging authorization behavior — they see the in-body check and may infer it is primary.
proposed_resolution: Code at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/author-agent-spec-tool.ts: strip the in-body writer.kind=='human' check (the auth-gate at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/auth-gate.ts is the canonical enforcement per FGAP-134/138 closure). Tool body trusts the (now-verified) writer field stamped by the auth-gate. Equivalent cleanup precedent landed at FGAP-138 closure (commit 3c9e8e4) for author-agent-spec / author-tool-grant / write-schema-migration; this gap is the inverse cleanup-not-yet-applied audit covering whether the cleanup at FGAP-138 actually stripped all sites or if any in-body check remains.
